heading: Taxes on political expenditures of section 501(c)(3) organizations
label: 4955 Taxes on political expenditures of section 501(c)(3) organizations
notes: Amendments1996—Subsec. (e). substituted “sections 4945 and 4958” for “section 4945” in heading and inserted “or an excess benefit for purposes of section 4958” before period at end of text.Pub. L. 104–168

children.heading: On the organization
children.label: 1 On the organization
children.text: There is hereby imposed on each political expenditure by a section 501(c)(3) organization a tax equal to 10 percent of the amount thereof. The tax imposed by this paragraph shall be paid by the organization.

children.heading: On the management
children.label: 2 On the management
children.text: There is hereby imposed on the agreement of any organization manager to the making of any expenditure, knowing that it is a political expenditure, a tax equal to 2½ percent of the amount thereof, unless such agreement is not willful and is due to reasonable cause. The tax imposed by this paragraph shall be paid by any organization manager who agreed to the making of the expenditure.

children.heading: On the organization
children.label: 1 On the organization
children.text: In any case in which an initial tax is imposed by subsection (a)(1) on a political expenditure and such expenditure is not corrected within the taxable period, there is hereby imposed a tax equal to 100 percent of the amount of the expenditure. The tax imposed by this paragraph shall be paid by the organization.

children.heading: On the management
children.label: 2 On the management
children.text: In any case in which an additional tax is imposed by paragraph (1), if an organization manager refused to agree to part or all of the correction, there is hereby imposed a tax equal to 50 percent of the amount of the political expenditure. The tax imposed by this paragraph shall be paid by any organization manager who refused to agree to part or all of the correction.

children.heading: Joint and several liability
children.label: 1 Joint and several liability
children.text: If more than 1 person is liable under subsection (a)(2) or (b)(2) with respect to the making of a political expenditure, all such persons shall be jointly and severally liable under such subsection with respect to such expenditure.

children.heading: Limit for management
children.label: 2 Limit for management
children.text: With respect to any 1 political expenditure, the maximum amount of the tax imposed by subsection (a)(2) shall not exceed $5,000, and the maximum amount of the tax imposed by subsection (b)(2) shall not exceed $10,000.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “political expenditure” means any amount paid or incurred by a section 501(c)(3) organization in any participation in, or intervention in (including the publication or distribution of statements), any political campaign on behalf of (or in opposition to) any candidate for public office.

children.label: A
children.text: Amounts paid or incurred to such individual for speeches or other services.

children.label: C
children.text: Expenses of conducting polls, surveys, or other studies, or preparing papers or other materials, for use by such individual.

children.label: D
children.text: Expenses of advertising, publicity, and fundraising for such individual.

children.label: E
children.text: Any other expense which has the primary effect of promoting public recognition, or otherwise primarily accruing to the benefit, of such individual.

children.heading: Coordination with sections 4945 and 4958
children.label: e Coordination with sections 4945 and 4958
children.text: If tax is imposed under this section with respect to any political expenditure, such expenditure shall not be treated as a taxable expenditure for purposes of section 4945 or an excess benefit for purposes of section 4958.

children.heading: Section 501(c)(3) organization
children.label: 1 Section 501(c)(3) organization
children.text: The term “section 501(c)(3) organization” means any organization which (without regard to any political expenditure) would be described in section 501(c)(3) and exempt from taxation under section 501(a).

children.label: A
children.text: any officer, director, or trustee of the organization (or individual having powers or responsibilities similar to those of officers, directors, or trustees of the organization), and

children.label: B
children.text: with respect to any expenditure, any employee of the organization having authority or responsibility with respect to such expenditure.

children.heading: Correction
children.label: 3 Correction
children.text: The terms “correction” and “correct” mean, with respect to any political expenditure, recovering part or all of the expenditure to the extent recovery is possible, establishment of safeguards to prevent future political expenditures, and where full recovery is not possible, such additional corrective action as is prescribed by the Secretary by regulations.

children.label: A
children.text: the date of mailing a notice of deficiency under section 6212 with respect to the tax imposed by subsection (a)(1), or

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which tax imposed by subsection (a)(1) is assessed.
